reasoning: The CBOE Crude Oil Volatility Index (OVX) experienced a massive spike in late April 2025, peaking around 54.46 before rapidly mean-reverting down to 39.82 by May 9. Since volatility indices are structural mean-reverting instruments, the short-term momentum strongly suggests it will continue to fall toward its historical average (typically 25-35) in the coming weeks. For the nearest resolution date (May 18), the sharp downward momentum makes an increase highly unlikely (0.15). For June 10, mean reversion will likely be complete barring entirely new crises (0.20). Over longer horizons (August and November), the chance of a *new* geopolitical or macroeconomic shock inherently increases. Given that 39.82 is an elevated baseline compared to the historical median, my probability estimates start low and gradually increase toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon widens, strictly adhering to the instruction that uncertainty must increase for distant dates.
